meaning: n.自动钢琴,轻而易举之事
examples: Around 1900 the pianola is introduced. This instrument, which is played by perforated paper rolls, becomes a world-wide success.
John Adams conducts the L.A. Philharmonic's New Music Group in a performance of Grand Pianola Music.
One of Adams' most famous works, Grand Pianola Music, premiered at a new music festival in 1982.
